framing: Hotel operations manager returning after 2-year career break, applying to hospitality group in Singapore

jd.title: Director of Rooms — Luxury Segment

requirements.text: 10+ years of progressive experience in hotel operations, with at least 3 years as Rooms Division Manager or equivalent
Experience in luxury or upper-upscale hotel brands (Forbes 5-star or equivalent)
Strong knowledge of revenue management principles and dynamic pricing strategies
Experience with Opera PMS and revenue management systems (IDeaS or Duetto)
Proven track record of achieving guest satisfaction scores above brand benchmark (NPS, ReviewPro, or Medallia)
Experience managing departments of 80+ staff across front office, housekeeping, and concierge
Fluency in English; Mandarin or Japanese is a strong advantage
Degree in Hospitality Management or related field
Experience with luxury service training programs (Forbes Travel Guide, LQA)

responsibilities: Oversee all rooms division operations (front office, housekeeping, concierge, guest relations) for 527-room luxury property
Drive RevPAR performance in collaboration with Revenue Management and Sales
Maintain Forbes 5-star service standards across all guest touchpoints
Manage annual rooms division budget of SGD 12M
Lead recruitment, training, and retention strategy for 95-person rooms team
Champion digital guest experience initiatives (mobile check-in, digital concierge)

expected_output.summary: Luxury hotel operations leader with 11 years of experience at The Ritz-Carlton and Four Seasons, returning from a 2-year career break. Previously Rooms Division Manager at The Ritz-Carlton, Millenia Singapore managing 90-person team across a 608-room luxury property. Achieved highest guest satisfaction scores in Asia-Pacific region (2021). Deep expertise in Opera PMS, revenue management, and Forbes 5-star service standards. BSc Hotel Management from Ecole hoteliere de Lausanne.

experience.title: Rooms Division Manager
bullets: Managed rooms division (front office, housekeeping, concierge, guest relations) for 608-room Forbes 5-star property
Led team of 90 across 4 departments with annual staff budget of SGD 8.5M
Achieved highest Medallia guest satisfaction score in Ritz-Carlton Asia-Pacific portfolio (2021, 92.4/100)
Implemented IDeaS revenue management system, contributing to 12% RevPAR growth year-over-year
Oversaw COVID-19 safe management measures and government quarantine facility operations (2020-2021)

experience.title: Assistant Front Office Manager (promoted from Guest Relations Manager, 2016)
bullets: Promoted from Guest Relations Manager to Assistant Front Office Manager within 2 years
Managed front office team of 25 for 255-room luxury property
Led Forbes Travel Guide service training achieving 5-star rating retention for 4 consecutive years
Administered Opera PMS including rate configuration, guest profiles, and group block management

experience.title: Primary Caregiver — Family Relocation
bullets: Relocated with family to Japan (spouse assignment), managed international household transition
Completed Cornell University online certificate in Hotel Revenue Management (2024)
Maintained industry network through Singapore Hotel Association membership and regional conferences

certifications: Cornell University — Hotel Revenue Management Certificate (2024)
Forbes Travel Guide — Star Service Training (online programme)
education: BSc International Hospitality Management — Ecole hoteliere de Lausanne (2010-2014)

jd_requirements: 10+ years hotel operations 3+ Rooms Division Manager
departments 80+ staff front office housekeeping concierge
Forbes Travel Guide LQA luxury training
deliberate_flaws: No pre-opening or renovation experience (JD lists as nice-to-have)
No Mandarin or Japanese fluency (only English)
No digital guest experience implementation (mobile check-in, digital concierge)
Returner — hospitality industry changed significantly during break (AI concierge, contactless everything)
Budget SGD 8.5M vs JD's SGD 12M scope — scaling gap
